content:
# Final Review Proposal
**Producer:** agent:codex/gpt-5.4-xhigh

## Summary

Reviewed the branch against ca4d23414de261e5b7d9716c8c608569045ab18d, focusing on the changed product files under `src/` and `tests/`. Found 1 high-severity correctness issue in the checkpoint-tree logic; I did not run the Rust build/test commands because `cargo` is unavailable in this shell.

## Amendments

1. finding_summary: `build_checkpoint_tree` deletes tracked-but-gitignored files from checkpoint commits instead of preserving their parent blob. `src/adapters/worktree.rs:263-268` unconditionally `update-index --force-remove`s every tracked ignored path after `read-tree` loaded the parent tree, so a checkpoint/reset turns any still-tracked ignored file into a real deletion. The new assertions in `tests/unit/checkpoint_test.rs:352-385` and `tests/unit/checkpoint_test.rs:437-525` lock in that destructive behavior.
affected_files:
- src/adapters/worktree.rs:263-268
- tests/unit/checkpoint_test.rs:352-385
- tests/unit/checkpoint_test.rs:437-525
remediation_hint: Keep tracked gitignored paths at their parent/index entry in the temporary index, the same way assume-unchanged paths are restored, and compute the ignored set from the pre-staging tracked state so ignored edits/deletions do not leak without deleting the tracked file from the checkpoint tree.
   Rationale: The bead only requires checkpoint commits to stop recording ignored local changes. The current implementation suppresses those changes by dropping the whole path from the tree, which means `reset_to_checkpoint` will remove tracked files from the worktree. That is destructive and broader than the requested fix.
